app.title: OpenClaw Provider Configuration
app.subtitle: Configure model providers with custom API keys and endpoints

provider.noProviders: No providers configured yet. Add one to get started.

model.title: Discovered Models
model.name: Display Name
model.noModels: No models discovered. Fetch models from a provider first.

config.title: Generated Configuration
config.description: Copy this configuration into your openclaw.json file under the models.providers key.
config.empty: Configure at least one provider to generate config.
config.saveSuccess: Config saved and gateway reload triggered
config.reloadFailed: Gateway reload failed — may not be running
config.externalChangeDesc: The config file was modified outside this editor. Reload to get the latest version.
config.invalidJson: Invalid JSON — cannot render tree view
config.quickEditDesc: Edit common config values without touching JSON directly

env.title: System Environment Check
env.subtitle: Verify your system meets OpenClaw requirements

checks.nodeVersionDesc: OpenClaw requires Node ≥22.14.0. Node 24 is recommended.

tips.title: Optimization Tips
tips.nodeUpgrade: Upgrade to Node 24 for best performance and latest features.
tips.gatewayToken: Set OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN when binding beyond loopback for security.
tips.providerKey: Set at least one model provider API key (OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.).
tips.runDoctor: Run `openclaw doctor` for a comprehensive health check and auto-repair.
tips.shellEnv: Enable OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1 to auto-import API keys from your login shell.
tips.ollamaLocal: For local models, install Ollama: https://ollama.ai/download
tips.dockerSandbox: Install Docker for sandboxed tool execution.

install.title: Offline / Manual Installation
install.scriptDesc: The fastest way — auto-detects OS, installs Node if needed, and launches onboarding.
install.npmDesc: If you already have Node.js installed:

offlineSteps.step1: On a machine with internet, run: npm pack openclaw@latest
offlineSteps.step2: Transfer the .tgz file to the target machine
offlineSteps.step3: On the target machine: npm install -g openclaw-<version>.tgz

envSetupSteps.step2: Fill in your API keys and tokens

installer.title: One-Click Install
installer.subtitle: Choose an installation method and install OpenClaw with one click
installer.reinstall: You can reinstall or upgrade using any method below.

script.title: Installer Script
script.desc: Auto-detects OS, installs Node if needed. The fastest way to get started.

npm.title: npm Global
npm.desc: Install via npm package manager. Requires Node ≥22.14.0.

pnpm.title: pnpm Global
pnpm.desc: Install via pnpm. Requires pnpm and Node ≥22.14.0.

docker.title: Docker Container
docker.desc: Run in an isolated container. Requires Docker daemon running.

vagrant.title: Vagrant VM
vagrant.desc: Create a full VirtualBox VM with OpenClaw pre-installed. Supports Kali Linux and Windows 10.

source.title: From Source
source.desc: Clone and build from source. For contributors and custom builds.

source.chinaMirrorDesc: Use ghproxy.com to accelerate Git clone
source.zipSourceDesc: Download the official source archive from hunyuandata.cn instead of git clone
source.zipVersionHint: Enter a version tag (e.g. v2026.4.5) or leave as "latest"

installer.noOutput: No output yet. Click Install to begin.

docker.daemonHint: If all mirrors fail, configure Docker daemon mirrors directly:
docker.modeBuildDesc: Build from project Dockerfile locally, no network pull needed
docker.modePullDesc: Pull pre-built image from a registry, requires network access
docker.buildTagHint: Tag name for the locally built image, default openclaw:local

npm.registryHint: Leave empty for default. Use https://registry.npmmirror.com for China.

vagrant.kaliDesc: Rolling release, lightweight, SSH access. Box ~2GB.
vagrant.windowsDesc: Full desktop, RDP access. Box ~6GB, slower first boot.
vagrant.kaliNote: First run downloads the Kali box (~2GB). SSH into the VM with: vagrant ssh
vagrant.windowsNote: First run downloads the Windows 10 box (~6GB). Access via RDP: vagrant rdp. Enable GUI above to see the VirtualBox window.
vagrant.chinaMirrorDesc: Use USTC mirror for faster Vagrant box downloads in China (mirrors.ustc.edu.cn)

packager.selectDesc: Choose which platforms to generate installer packages for.
packager.envVarsHint: Comma-separated KEY=VALUE pairs baked into the install script.
packager.noPackages: No packages yet. Select targets and click Build.

uninstaller.title: Uninstall OpenClaw
uninstaller.subtitle: Remove OpenClaw and optionally clean up all data
uninstaller.notInstalled: OpenClaw does not appear to be installed.

npm.title: npm Package
npm.desc: Remove the globally installed npm package

pnpm.title: pnpm Package
pnpm.desc: Remove the globally installed pnpm package

docker.title: Docker Container & Image
docker.desc: Stop and remove the Docker container and image

vagrant.title: Vagrant VMs
vagrant.desc: Destroy all Vagrant VMs (Kali and Windows) and remove Vagrantfiles

all.title: Full Uninstall
all.desc: Remove all: npm, pnpm, Docker, Vagrant VMs, stop gateway, remove services

uninstaller.removeDataWarn: This will permanently delete all config, sessions, credentials, and workspace data.
uninstaller.confirm: Are you sure? This action cannot be undone.

versions.title: Version Manager
versions.subtitle: Fetch and install OpenClaw from official source zips (hunyuandata.cn)

gateway.title: Gateway Manager
gateway.noLogs: No log entries — log file may not exist yet
gateway.dashboardDesc: Open the OpenClaw web dashboard to chat and manage the gateway
